subcommands.name: component-issues
subcommands.description: List available component-issues

subcommands.name: tag
subcommands.description:
Create an immutable and exportable component snapshot, tagged with a release version.
If no patterns are provided, it will tag all new and modified components.
If patterns are entered, you can specify a version per pattern using "@" sign, e.g. bit tag foo@1.0.0 bar@minor baz@major

bit tag [component-patterns...]

options.description: A log message describing the latest changes
Include unmodified components (by default, only new and modified components are tagged)
Open an editor to write a tag message for each component. Optionally, specify the editor-name (defaults to vim)
Tag with the given version
Options are: [major, premajor, minor, preminor, patch, prepatch, prerelease], default to patch
Prerelease identifier (e.g. "dev" to get "1.0.0-dev.1")
Syntactic sugar for "--increment patch"
Syntactic sugar for "--increment minor"
Syntactic sugar for "--increment major"
Syntactic sugar for "--increment prerelease" and --prerelease-id <identifier>
Tag only components whose head is a snap (not a tag)
Complete a merge process by tagging the unmerged components
Skip running component tests during tag process
Skip auto tagging dependents
Do not persist. Only keep note of the changes to be made
Persist the changes generated by --soft tag
Skip the tag pipeline to avoid publishing the components
Proceed to tag pipeline even when build pipeline fails
(default to 1) increment semver flag (patch/minor/major) by. e.g. incrementing patch by 2: 0.0.1 -> 0.0.3

Ignore component issues (shown in "bit status" as "issues found"), issues to ignore: [MissingPackagesDependenciesOnFs, MissingManuallyConfiguredPackages, MissingComponents, UntrackedDependencies, ResolveErrors, RelativeComponents, RelativeComponentsAuthored, ParseErrors, MissingDists, LegacyInsideHarmony, MissingDependenciesOnFs, ImportNonMainFiles, MultipleEnvs, MissingLinksFromNodeModulesToSrc, CircularDependencies, DuplicateComponentAndPackage, MergeConfigHasConflict, NonLoadedEnv, ExternalEnvWithoutVersion, RemovedDependencies] to ignore multiple issues, separate them by a comma and wrap with quotes. To ignore all issues, specify '*'
Allow tagging even when the component has newer versions e.g. for hotfixes (default = false)
Stop pipeline execution on the first failed task (by default a task is skipped only when its dependency failed)
Not needed for now. Run the pipeline build and complete the tag
DEPRECATED (not needed anymore, it is the default now). Tag all new and modified components
DEPRECATED (use "--unmodified" instead). Tag all components of the local scope
DEPRECATED (use "--skip-tests", "--ignore-build-errors" or "--unmodified" instead). Force-tag even if tests are failing and even when component has not changed
DEPRECATED. Use --disable-tag-pipeline instead

args.name: arg
args.description: Component name, component id, or component pattern. Use component pattern to select multiple components. Use comma to separate patterns and "!" to exclude. e.g. "ui/**, !ui/button" wrap the pattern with quotes. By default, all new and modified are tagged

In case of a conflict, resolve according to the strategy: [ours, theirs, manual]
In case of an unresolved merge, revert to pre-merge state
Mark an unresolved merge as resolved and create a new snap with the changes
Do not auto snap even if the merge completed without conflicts
In case of snap during the merge, run the build-pipeline
Show details of components that were not merged successfully
Do not install new dependencies resulting from the merge
Override the default message for the auto snap

DEPRECATED. Use --auto-merge-resolve. In case of a conflict, override local with incoming changes

Relevant for merging lanes into main, which by default squashes all lane snaps
EXPERIMENTAL. Relevant for merging a lane into another non-main lane, which by default does not squash
Allow merging when components are modified due to config changes (such as dependencies) only and not files
Show details of components that were not merged successfully
Do not install dependencies of the imported components
Use the local state of target-lane if exits locally, without updating it from the remote
Relevant for "--pattern" and "--workspace". Merge also dependencies of the specified components
Relevant when a component on a lane and the component on main have nothing in common. merge-strategy can be "ours" (default) or "theirs"
When merging main, include workspace components that are not on the lane (by default only lane components are merged)

args.name: lane
args.description: Lane-name or full lane-id (if remote) to merge into the current lane

args.name: pattern
args.description: Partially merge the lane - only components that match the specified component-pattern. Component pattern format: component name, component id, or component pattern. Use component pattern to select multiple components. Use comma to separate patterns and "!" to exclude. E.g. "ui/**, !ui/button". Wrap the pattern with quotes

subcommands.name: add-readme
subcommands.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Sets an existing component as the readme of a lane

args.name: component-id
args.description: The component name or id of the component to use as the lane's readme

args.name: lane-name
args.description: The lane to attach the readme to (defaults to the current lane)

subcommands.name: remove-readme
subcommands.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Remove lane readme component

args.name: laneName
args.description: Name of the lane from which the readme component should be removed

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import a remote lane to your workspace and switch to that lane

options.description: Do not install dependencies of the imported components
Import only components from the lane that fit the specified component-pattern to the workspace. Works only when the workspace is empty

args.name: lane
args.description: The remote lane name to be imported

subcommands.name: remove-comp
subcommands.description: DEPRECATED. Remove components when on a lane

options.description: Do not mark the components as removed from the lane. Instead, remove them from the workspace only
EXPERIMENTAL. Remove, i.e., delete, component/s on the main lane after merging this lane into the main

args.name: component-pattern
args.description: Component name, component id, or component pattern. Use component pattern to select multiple components. Use comma to separate patterns and "!" to exclude. E.g. "ui/**, !ui/button". Wrap the pattern with quotes

subcommands.name: log-file
subcommands.description: EXPERIMENTAL: Show file history

options.description: Show each log entry in one line

args.name: arg
args.description: File path relative to the workspace

subcommands.name: mini-status
subcommands.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Basic status for fast execution

options.description: Show component issues
Output in JSON format

args.name: arg
args.description: Component name, component ID, or component pattern

subcommands.name: pattern
subcommands.description: List the component ids matching the given pattern

options.description: Return the output as JSON

subcommands.name: recover
subcommands.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Recover component(s) soft-deleted from the workspace, or a remote scope

options.description: Do not install packages in case of importing components

subcommands.name: revert
subcommands.description: Replace the current component files by the specified version, leave the version intact

options.description: Showing verbose output for inspection
Do not install packages of the imported components

subcommands.name: scope
subcommands.description:
Manage the scope-name for components

bit scope <sub-command>

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description:
Sets the scope for specified component/s. If no component is specified, sets the default scope of the workspace. Default scopes for components are set in the bitmap file. The default scope for a workspace is set in the workspace.jsonc. A component is set with a scope (as opposed to default scope) only once it is versioned. You can use a pattern for multiple component ids, such as bit scope set scope-name "org.scope/utils/**". Use comma to separate patterns and '!' to exclude. Always wrap the pattern with single quotes to avoid collision with shell commands.

Usage: scope set <scope-name> [component-pattern]

args.name: arg
args.description: Name of the scope to set

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description:
Renames the scope name for all components with the specified 'current scope name' - only available for new components that have not yet been snapped/tagged. If <current-scope-name> is also the defaultScope for the workspace, this command will set <new-scope-name> as the defaultScope instead.

Usage: scope rename <current-scope-name> <new-scope-name>

options.description: Update the import statements in all dependent components

subcommands.name: rename-owner
subcommands.description:
Renames the owner part of the scope-name for all components with the specified 'current owner name'.

Usage: scope rename-owner <current-owner-name> <new-owner-name>

options.description: Update the import statements in all dependent components
EXPERIMENTAL. Use AST to transform files instead of regex

subcommands.name: fork
subcommands.description:
Fork all components of the original-scope and refactor the source-code to use the new scope name.

Usage: scope fork <original-scope> <new-scope>

options.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Use AST to transform files instead of regex

subcommands.name: server
subcommands.description:
EXPERIMENTAL. Communicate with bit CLI program via HTTP requests

bit server

options.description: Port to run the server on

subcommands.name: system
subcommands.description:
System operations

bit system <sub-command>

subcommands.name: log
subcommands.description:
Print debug.log to the screen

Usage: system log

subcommands.name: snap
subcommands.description:
Create an immutable and exportable component snapshot (non-release version)

bit snap [component-pattern]

options.description: Snap message describing the latest changes - will appear in component history log
Include unmodified components (by default, only new and modified components are snapped)
Complete a merge process by snapping the unmerged components
Not needed for now. Run the build pipeline locally in case the feature-flag build-on-ci is enabled
Open an editor to write a snap message per component. Optionally specify the editor-name (defaults to vim)
Skip running component tests during snap process
Skip auto snapping dependents
Skip the snap pipeline. This will for instance skip packing and publishing component version for install, and app deployment
Proceed to snap pipeline even when build pipeline fails

Ignore component issues (shown in "bit status" as "issues found"), issues to ignore: [MissingPackagesDependenciesOnFs, MissingManuallyConfiguredPackages, MissingComponents, UntrackedDependencies, ResolveErrors, RelativeComponents, RelativeComponentsAuthored, ParseErrors, MissingDists, LegacyInsideHarmony, MissingDependenciesOnFs, ImportNonMainFiles, MultipleEnvs, MissingLinksFromNodeModulesToSrc, CircularDependencies, DuplicateComponentAndPackage, MergeConfigHasConflict, NonLoadedEnv, ExternalEnvWithoutVersion, RemovedDependencies] to ignore multiple issues, separate them by a comma and wrap with quotes. To ignore all issues, specify "*"
DEPRECATED (not needed anymore, now the default). Snap all new and modified components
Stop pipeline execution on the first failed task (by default a task is skipped only when its dependency failed)
DEPRECATED (use "--skip-tests" or "--unmodified" instead). Force-snap even if tests are failing and even when component has not changed

args.name: arg
args.description: Component name, component ID, or component pattern. Use component pattern to select multiple components. Use comma to separate patterns and "!" to exclude. E.g. "ui/**, !ui/button". Wrap the pattern with quotes. By default, only new and modified components are snapped (add --unmodified to snap all components in the workspace)

subcommands.name: watch
subcommands.description:
Automatically recompile modified components (on save). By default, the watcher doesn't use polling, to keep the CPU idle. In some rare cases, this could result in missing file events (files are not watched). To fix it, try to stop other watchers on the same machine. Alternatively, to use polling, run "bit config set watch_use_polling true".

bit watch

options.description: Show all watch events and compiler verbose output
Skip compilation step before starting to watch
EXPERIMENTAL. Show errors/warnings for types. Options are [file, project] to investigate only changed file or entire project. Defaults to project

subcommands.name: use
subcommands.description:
Set aspects in the workspace/scope config to make them loadable by the workspace/scope.

bit use <component-id>

args.name: arg
args.description: The component ID of the aspect

subcommands.name: ws-config
subcommands.description:
Manage workspace config files.

bit ws-config <sub-command>

subcommands.name: ws-config write
subcommands.description:
EXPERIMENTAL. Write config files in the workspace. Useful for IDEs.

ws-config write

options.description: Delete existing config files from the workspace. Highly recommended to run it with "--dry-run" first
Only write config files for the given writers. Use comma to separate multiple writers. Use ws-config list to see all writers
Do not prompt for confirmation
Write configs inside each one of the component's dir, avoid deduping
Show the paths that configs will be written per env
Use with --json flag. Show the config content and the paths that will be written per env
Showing verbose output for writing
JSON format

subcommands.name: ws-config clean
subcommands.description:
EXPERIMENTAL. Clean (delete) written config files in the workspace. Useful for IDEs.

ws-config clean

options.description: Do not prompt for confirmation
Only clean config files for the given writers. Use comma to separate multiple writers. Use ws-config list to see all writers
Show the paths of configs that will be cleaned
JSON format

subcommands.name: ws-config list
subcommands.description:
EXPERIMENTAL. List config writers.

ws-config list

options.description: JSON format
Show version
Show help for bit
